name: Unitel (Star Telecom)
description: Unitel, the trademark of Star Telecom, is the largest mobile operator in Laos by subscribers, revenue and network infrastructure. It is a joint venture between Vietnam's Viettel Group and Lao Asia Telecom, launched in October 2009.
metaDescription: Unitel (Star Telecom) is the largest mobile network operator in Laos, a Viettel–Lao Asia Telecom joint venture launched in 2009.
address: Nongbone Road, Phonxay Village, Saysettha District, Vientiane Capital, Laos

name: Lao Telecom (LTC)
description: Lao Telecom Company (LTC / LaoTel) is a leading telecommunications operator in Laos, offering mobile, fixed-line and broadband internet services. It is a joint venture between the Lao government (51%) and Shenington Investments (49%).
metaDescription: Lao Telecom (LTC) is a leading Lao telecom offering mobile, fixed-line and broadband services; a government–Shenington joint venture.
address: Lanexang Avenue, Chanthabouly District, Vientiane, Laos

name: BCEL Bank
description: Banque Pour Le Commerce Exterieur Lao (BCEL) is a leading state-owned commercial bank in Laos, established in 1975. It operates over 50 branches nationwide and was the first company listed on the Lao Securities Exchange in 2010.
metaDescription: BCEL is a leading state-owned commercial bank in Laos (est. 1975) with 50+ branches and the BCEL One digital wallet.
address: No 1, Pangkham Road, Chanthabouly District, Vientiane Capital, Laos

name: Phongsavanh Bank
description: Phongsavanh Bank (PSVB), established on 29 March 2007, is the first and leading private commercial bank in Lao PDR, offering integrated financial services and holding ISO 9001:2015 accreditation in its banking operations.
metaDescription: Phongsavanh Bank (est. 2007) is the first and leading private commercial bank in Laos, offering integrated financial services.
address: Unit 09, Rue Samsenthai, Chanthabouly District, Vientiane, Laos

name: Lao Airlines
description: Lao Airlines is the state-owned flag carrier of Laos, headquartered at Wattay International Airport in Vientiane. Founded in 1976, it operates domestic and international routes to destinations including Cambodia, China, South Korea, Thailand and Vietnam.
metaDescription: Lao Airlines is the state-owned flag carrier of Laos (founded 1976), flying domestic and regional routes from Wattay International Airport.
address: Wattay International Airport, Souphanouvong Avenue, Vientiane, Laos

name: Crowne Plaza Vientiane
description: Crowne Plaza Vientiane is an IHG business and luxury hotel in central Vientiane, Laos, with 198 guest rooms and suites, located about 15 minutes from Wattay International Airport.
metaDescription: Crowne Plaza Vientiane is an IHG business hotel in central Vientiane with 198 rooms, dining and meeting facilities.
address: 20 Samsenthai Road, Nongduang Nua Village, Vientiane 01000, Laos

name: Settha Palace Hotel
description: Settha Palace Hotel is a luxury boutique hotel in a restored 1932 French colonial building in central Vientiane, Laos. Nationalised after 1975, it was restored and reopened in 1999.
metaDescription: Settha Palace Hotel is a luxury boutique hotel in a restored 1932 French colonial building in central Vientiane, reopened 1999.

name: Électricité du Laos (EDL)
description: Électricité du Laos (EDL) is the state-owned power corporation of Laos, owning and operating the country's electricity generation, transmission and distribution assets and managing power import and export. Headquartered in Vientiane.
metaDescription: Électricité du Laos (EDL) is the state-owned power utility of Laos, managing generation, transmission, distribution and power trade.
address: Lao-Thai Friendship Road, Thongkang Village, Sisattanak District, Vientiane, Laos

name: Lao Brewery Co. (Beerlao)
description: Lao Brewery Co., Ltd. (LBC), established in 1973, is the leading beverage company in Laos and maker of Beerlao. A joint venture involving the Carlsberg Group and the Lao government, based on Thadeua Road, Vientiane.
metaDescription: Lao Brewery Co. (est. 1973) is the maker of Beerlao, the national beer of Laos; a Carlsberg–Lao government joint venture.
address: Km 12 Thadeua Road, Salakham Village, Hatsayfong District, Vientiane, Laos

name: Kolao Group
description: Kolao Group is a Laos-based conglomerate founded in 1997, one of the country's largest private enterprises. Originating in automotive distribution (Hyundai, Kia and others), it has diversified into real estate, energy and trading. Headquartered at Kolao Tower, Vientiane.
metaDescription: Kolao Group (founded 1997) is one of Laos's largest private conglomerates, spanning automotive, real estate, energy and trading.
address: Kolao Tower, 23 Singha Road, Nongbone Village, Saysettha District, Vientiane, Laos

name: Loca Laos
description: LOCA is Laos's leading homegrown ride-hailing and e-mobility super app, founded in 2018. It offers taxi booking, EV rides, digital payments and eSIM services across Vientiane, Luang Prabang, Vang Vieng, Pakse and Savannakhet.
metaDescription: LOCA (founded 2018) is Laos's leading homegrown ride-hailing and e-mobility super app, with EV rides and digital payments.
address: 4th Floor, Vientiane Center, Khouvieng Road, Vientiane, Laos

name: Sitthi Logistics
description: Sitthi Logistics Lao Co., Ltd. is a Phongsavanh Group logistics company providing domestic and cross-border transport of fuel, petroleum and consumer goods across Laos, Thailand, Vietnam and China, with involvement in the Vientiane Logistics Park and Thanaleng Dry Port.
metaDescription: Sitthi Logistics (Phongsavanh Group) provides domestic and cross-border fuel and freight transport across Laos, Thailand, Vietnam and China.
address: Kamphengmeuang Road (Phonthan), Phonthan Village, Saysettha District, Vientiane Capital, Laos

name: Vientiane Center
description: Vientiane Center is a major shopping and entertainment complex on Khouvieng Road, Vientiane, opened in March 2015. It houses international and local retail brands, a cineplex, food court, bowling and a children's playground, plus an adjoining office tower.
metaDescription: Vientiane Center is a major shopping and entertainment mall on Khouvieng Road (opened 2015) with retail, cineplex, food court and bowling.
address: Khouvieng Road, Nongchan Village, Sisattanak District, Vientiane, Laos

name: Phu Bia Mining
description: Phu Bia Mining Limited is a 90%-owned subsidiary of Australia's PanAust, operating the Phu Kham Copper-Gold and Ban Houayxai Gold-Silver operations in Laos. Its head office is at Capital Tower, 23 Singha Road, Vientiane.
metaDescription: Phu Bia Mining (a PanAust subsidiary) operates the Phu Kham copper-gold and Ban Houayxai gold-silver mines in Laos.
businessType: Gold, copper and silver mining company
address: 7th Floor, Capital Tower, 23 Singha Road, Ban Phonxay, Saysettha District, Vientiane, Laos

name: Ministry of Industry and Commerce
description: The Ministry of Industry and Commerce (MoIC) of Lao PDR is the government body responsible for governing and developing industrial and commercial activity, regulating and promoting manufacturing, trade, and import/export in Laos. Located on Phonxay Road, Vientiane.
metaDescription: The Ministry of Industry and Commerce (MoIC) of Lao PDR governs trade, commerce, industry regulation and business registration.
address: Phonxay Road, Ban Phonxay, Saysettha District, Vientiane Capital, Laos
